body:
]> 14/07/2026 7:33 Snowstorm slowly clearing at Perisher.

More winter weather is on the cards as the last of the storm rolls through Perisher. We've picked up another 3cm on the snowstake since yesterday, taking the storm total to 28cm. Hopefully there's still more to come too, with scattered showers forecast to fall as snow on the upper slopes throughout the day.

Expect a cloudy day and a top of 2 degrees, with westerly winds building to 28km/h and stronger gusts likely. High winds may impact lift operations, so be sure to download the Perisher App or keep an eye on the lift report for real-time updates on lift wind holds. Rest assured, the team are out working hard behind the scenes to open more lifts and terrain when it's safe to do so.

It's a big week on the hill, with a warm welcome to all our guests competing in the Sydney Interschools! We wish you the best of luck in your races. To our regular guests, please note that racing kicks off today on Front and Centre Valley, so it might be a good idea to explore some of our other resort areas.

As the sun goes down, the fun continues, with night skiing and boarding running from 6:00pm to 8:30pm. Guests can ski and ride under the stars, capped off with a special fireworks display (weather dependent). NSW Fire and Rescue will also be down at the bottom of the V8, so be sure to pay them a visit if you're out and about this evening!

Looking ahead, this storm is expected to ease tomorrow as a change moves through, clearing the way for sunnier skies later in the week.

Guthega Road: Road open to Guthega.

Alpine Way: Road open to Bullocks Flat, Skitube.

]]> 53.4 3 28 20 0 21 *Natural snow depth is supplemented with snowmaking in certain areas. Natural snow depth is measured at roughly 1,830m by Snowy Hydro Limited who have invested in new instrumentation across the Snowy Scheme and can now provide more frequent snow depth readings at a site close to the Spencers Creek snow course. This report reflects conditions at the date and time of this report. Conditions, lifts status and operations subsequent to this report may differ.
